chapter_title: Chapter 4: Regulatory Aspects of Insurance Agents

questions.text: What is the minimum age required to become an insurance agent in India?
options: 16 Years
18 Years
21 Years
25 Years
questions.explanation: To apply for an insurance agency license, an individual must have completed 18 years of age (must be a major).

questions.text: What is the minimum educational qualification required to become an insurance agent?
options: 8th Pass
10th Pass (Matriculation)
12th Pass
Graduate
questions.explanation: As per IRDAI guidelines, the minimum educational qualification to act as an insurance agent is passing the 10th standard or equivalent examination.

questions.text: An insurance agent's license is valid for a period of how many years?
options: 1 Year
3 Years
5 Years
Lifetime
questions.explanation: Once issued, an insurance agent's license remains valid for a period of 3 years. It must be renewed before its expiry.

questions.text: Which section of the Insurance Act, 1938 strictly prohibits the offering or accepting of rebates?
options: Section 38
Section 39
Section 41
Section 45
questions.explanation: Section 41 of the Insurance Act, 1938 strictly prohibits any person from offering or receiving a rebate (a discount or passing on of commission) as an inducement to buy an insurance policy.

questions.text: What is the maximum penalty for a person found guilty of offering a rebate under Section 41?
options: Rs. 1 Lakh
Rs. 5 Lakhs
Rs. 10 Lakhs
Imprisonment up to 1 year
questions.explanation: Any default in complying with the provisions of Section 41 (Prohibition of Rebates) is punishable with a fine which may extend up to Rs. 10 Lakhs.

questions.text: Under the IRDAI rules, can a person act as an agent for two different Life Insurance companies simultaneously?
options: Yes, if they get permission from IRDAI
No, it is strictly prohibited
Yes, they can represent up to three life insurers
Yes, but only in different states
questions.explanation: An agent cannot hold a license for more than one insurer in the SAME category. They cannot work for two life insurers, but they can work for one life and one general insurer (Composite Agent).

questions.text: According to the Code of Conduct, what must an agent do if a prospect asks about the commission the agent will earn?
options: Politely refuse to answer as it is confidential
Lie about the amount to avoid losing the sale
Disclose the scale of commission if asked
Complain to the insurance company
questions.explanation: As per the IRDAI Code of Conduct, an agent is obligated to disclose the scale of commission in respect of the policy if explicitly asked by the prospect.

questions.text: Which of the following acts is considered a violation of the Code of Conduct for an insurance agent?
options: Showing the ID card to the customer
Explaining the policy terms clearly
Forcing a customer to terminate an existing policy to buy a new one
Helping the customer fill out the proposal form
questions.explanation: Forcing or inducing a prospect to cancel an existing policy within 3 years to buy a new one is an unethical practice known as 'Twisting' or 'Churning' and is strictly prohibited.

questions.text: What does a 'Composite Agent' mean?
options: An agent selling mutual funds and insurance
An agent who acts for one life insurer, one general insurer, and one health insurer
An agent who only sells group insurance
An agent representing multiple life insurance companies
questions.explanation: A Composite Agent is one who holds a license to act as an agent for one life insurer, one general insurer, one standalone health insurer, and one mono-line insurer simultaneously.

questions.text: Which of the following is a valid ground for the disqualification of an insurance agent?
options: Not meeting sales targets for one month
Being of unsound mind
Having multiple children
Working part-time as a teacher
questions.explanation: A person is disqualified from acting as an agent if they are found to be of unsound mind by a competent court, are a minor, or have been convicted of criminal misappropriation or fraud.

questions.text: As per the Code of Conduct, what is the agent's responsibility regarding the Prospectus?
options: To keep it hidden from the customer
To charge money for showing it
To bring the prospectus to the notice of the prospect and explain it
To submit it to the police station
questions.explanation: An agent must bring to the notice of the prospect all the terms, conditions, and benefits mentioned in the Prospectus to ensure transparency.

questions.text: If an agent's license is cancelled or suspended, to whom must the agent return their identity card?
options: IRDAI Head Office
The Designated Official / Insurance Company
The Police
The Policyholder
questions.explanation: If a license is suspended or cancelled, the agent must surrender their appointment letter and identity card to the designated official of the insurance company.

questions.text: Can an insurance agent share a part of their commission with the client to finalize a sale?
options: Yes, it is a common business practice
Yes, but only up to 10%
No, it is a violation of Section 41 (Rebate)
Only during festive seasons
questions.explanation: Sharing commission is explicitly defined as offering a rebate, which is illegal under Section 41 of the Insurance Act.

questions.text: What is the agent's duty when helping a customer fill out the proposal form?
options: Fill in the details themselves without asking the customer
Hide the customer's medical history to ensure the policy is issued
Explain the questions and ensure all material facts are truthfully disclosed
Sign the form on behalf of the customer
questions.explanation: The agent must assist the prospect in filling out the form properly and impress upon them the necessity of complete and truthful disclosure of all material facts.

questions.text: Who has the authority to issue an appointment letter to an individual to act as an insurance agent?
options: The Reserve Bank of India
The State Government
An Insurer (Insurance Company)
A senior agent
questions.explanation: Under the current regulations, an individual is appointed as an agent directly by the Insurer (Insurance Company), which issues them an appointment letter to solicit business.

questions.text: Is it mandatory for an agent to show their agency identity card?
options: No, it is optional
Yes, only to the police
Yes, on demand by the prospect
Yes, only at the time of claim settlement
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct mandates that an agent must carry their identity card and exhibit it to the prospect on demand.

questions.text: Which of the following would NOT disqualify a person from becoming an insurance agent?
options: Conviction for criminal misappropriation
Being a minor (under 18)
Not having a high-paying regular job
Being found guilty of fraud
questions.explanation: Employment status or wealth is not a criterion for disqualification. Fraud, minority, and criminal convictions are legal disqualifications.

questions.text: An agent tells a prospect that the policy will double their money in 3 years, which is factually incorrect. This act violates the Code of Conduct because:
options: The agent is not allowed to talk about money
The agent made a false promise/misrepresentation
The time limit should be 5 years
The agent should have asked the manager first
questions.explanation: An agent must not make any statements or promises that are false, misleading, or not guaranteed in the actual policy document.

questions.text: What action should an agent take if the insurance company rejects a proposal?
options: Fight with the underwriter
Keep the premium money
Inform the prospect about the rejection and ensure the premium is refunded
Secretly apply in another company using the same forms
questions.explanation: Professional conduct dictates that the agent must promptly inform the client if the risk is declined and facilitate the refund of any deposit paid.

questions.text: What is the agent's responsibility regarding a policy's renewal?
options: No responsibility once the sale is made
To pay the renewal premium from their own pocket
To advise the policyholder to let the policy lapse and buy a new one
To render necessary assistance and remind the policyholder to remit renewal premiums
questions.explanation: An agent's duty extends beyond the initial sale. They must assist the policyholder with services like renewals, nominations, and claims.

questions.text: An agent is found guilty of violating the Code of Conduct. Who can suspend or cancel their appointment?
options: The Police
The Policyholder
The Designated Official of the Insurer
The Consumer Court
questions.explanation: The Designated Official of the Insurance Company who appointed the agent has the authority to suspend or cancel the appointment if a violation is proven.

questions.text: According to the Code of Conduct, how should an agent handle their competitors?
options: Criticize their products openly
Make false statements about competitor's financial stability
Not interfere with proposals introduced by other agents
Steal their clients by offering rebates
questions.explanation: An agent must maintain healthy competition. They are strictly prohibited from interfering with proposals brought in by other agents or badmouthing competitors.

questions.text: If an agent collects a premium from a customer in cash, what is their immediate duty?
options: Deposit it in their personal bank account
Keep it safely at home for a week
Remit it to the insurance company immediately without any delay
Use it to pay for office expenses
questions.explanation: Any premium collected by an agent is held in 'Trust' and must be remitted to the insurance company immediately without any unauthorized delay.

questions.text: When can an agent demand their commission?
options: Before filling the proposal form
From the customer directly
From the insurer only after the policy is successfully issued
When the customer goes for a medical test
questions.explanation: Commission is paid by the insurance company to the agent, and it becomes payable only after the proposal is accepted and the policy is issued.

questions.text: What is the primary objective of IRDAI imposing a strict Code of Conduct on agents?
options: To limit the number of agents in the market
To ensure agents earn less money
To protect the interests of policyholders and maintain professional standards
To make the exam more difficult
questions.explanation: The entire regulatory framework and Code of Conduct are designed primarily to protect the policyholders from mis-selling and to ensure high ethical standards in the industry.
